title: Valve's new Steam Machine verification system is silent on these Steam Deck-busters
gist: Valve's new Steam Machine Verified rating system mechanically inherits Steam Deck results but leaves dozens of graphically demanding, popular titles marked 'Unknown' at launch, undercutting the value of the rating for new hardware buyers.

insights.claim: Valve's Steam Machine Verified program, rolled out to Steam store pages, marks dozens of graphically demanding games as 'Unknown' compatibility even though Valve already confirmed they run on SteamOS, because the Steam Deck (which the ratings pipeline seems to lean on) is too weak to have tested them at target settings.
insights.speaker: Kyle Orland
insights.so_what: New Steam Machine buyers can't rely on the badge system to know if flagship titles will run well, creating early friction and negative word-of-mouth risk for the hardware launch.
insights.quote: we’ve found that Valve is frustratingly “still learning about” Steam Machine compatibility for dozens of games that the Steam Deck is too weak to run capably.

insights.claim: Valve's default performance bar for Steam Machine verification is 1080p at 30fps, a step up from the Steam Deck's 1200x800 at 30fps standard.
insights.speaker: Kyle Orland
insights.so_what: The higher resolution bar is why many Steam Deck-unsupported titles could plausibly pass on Steam Machine, making the 'Unknown' backlog especially costly to leave untested.
insights.locator: paragraph beginning 'The Steam Machine compatibility for many Steam games'

insights.claim: At least twenty major, frequently top-selling titles -- including Black Myth Wukong, Starfield, Horizon: Forbidden West, Elden Ring Nightreign, Dragon's Dogma 2 and Stalker 2 -- run on SteamOS but are marked 'Unsupported' on Steam Deck for graphics reasons and 'Unknown' on Steam Machine.
insights.speaker: Kyle Orland
insights.so_what: These are exactly the AAA titles most likely to drive a purchase decision for living-room hardware, so leaving them untested is a high-visibility gap.
insights.quote: we do not currently have further information regarding Steam Machine compatibility.
insights.locator: list of SteamOS-compatible games with 'Unknown' Steam Machine compatibility

insights.claim: Games downgraded on Steam Deck purely because on-screen text is too small to read (e.g. 007: First Light, Lies of P) are automatically upgraded to 'Verified' on Steam Machine, since a TV screen resolves the text-legibility problem.
insights.speaker: Kyle Orland
insights.so_what: Shows the rating system can meaningfully differentiate device context when Valve has actually done the work, which makes the untested 'Unknown' backlog look more like a resourcing gap than a technical limitation.
insights.locator: paragraph beginning 'There is some good news for Steam Machine users'

insights.claim: Games downgraded to 'Playable' on Steam Deck because they require an on-screen keyboard carry a similar warning on Steam Machine, meaning couch users may still need a physical wireless keyboard on hand.
insights.speaker: Kyle Orland
insights.so_what: Undercuts the pure living-room, controller-only pitch for Steam Machine for a subset of the library.
insights.locator: paragraph beginning 'There is some good news for Steam Machine users'
